description: CLI tool for Ralph Team Agents. Create PRDs, trigger team runs, and check progress.

epics.title: ralph-team-agents CLI
epics.description: Build a TypeScript CLI that wraps the Ralph Team Agents system with three commands: init (create PRD), run (trigger ralph loop), status (show progress)

userStories.title: Project setup and CLI skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a TypeScript project with a CLI entry point so I can build commands on top of it.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with name 'ralph-team-agents', bin field pointing to dist/index.js
TypeScript with strict mode, compiles with npm run build
Dependencies: commander, chalk
Dev dependencies: typescript, tsx, @types/node
src/index.ts with Commander program that shows help with --help
npm run dev runs via tsx, npm run build compiles to dist/
Typecheck passes with npm run typecheck

userStories.title: status command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the current progress of my epics and stories so I know what's done and what's left.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents status [path] reads a prd.json file (default: ./prd.json)
Displays each epic with its status (pending/completed/partial/failed)
Under each epic, lists user stories with pass/fail status
Shows summary: X/Y stories passed, Z epics completed
Uses chalk colors: green for passed, red for failed, yellow for pending
Shows helpful error if prd.json not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: init command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a prd.json interactively so I don't have to write JSON by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents init starts interactive prompts
Asks for: project name, branch name, epic title, epic description
Asks to add user stories one at a time (title, description, acceptance criteria)
Asks 'Add another story?' after each story, stops when user says no
Generates valid prd.json in current directory
Generated JSON matches the prd.json schema (epics array, userStories with id/title/description/acceptanceCriteria/priority/passes)
Auto-generates IDs (EPIC-001, US-001, US-002, etc.)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: run command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to trigger the ralph loop from the CLI so I don't have to call ralph.sh directly.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents run [path] triggers ralph.sh with the given prd.json
Defaults to ./prd.json if no path given
Locates ralph.sh relative to the CLI package (bundled with it)
Passes through stdout/stderr from ralph.sh in real-time
Exits with ralph.sh's exit code
Shows helpful error if prd.json not found
Shows helpful error if claude CLI is not installed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Global install and README
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install ralph-team-agents globally so I can use it from any project.
acceptanceCriteria: npm link successfully makes ralph-team-agents available globally
ralph-team-agents --help shows all three commands with descriptions
ralph-team-agents --version shows package version
README.md with: what it is, install instructions, usage for each command, prd.json format example
Typecheck passes

epics.title: CLI Monitoring and Logs
epics.description: Add monitoring, log viewing, and epic management commands so users can track and control team runs

userStories.title: logs command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view the progress log so I can see what the teams have done.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents logs shows the contents of progress.txt
Supports --tail N flag to show last N entries
Colorizes output: green for PASS, red for FAIL, yellow for headings
Shows helpful message if progress.txt doesn't exist yet
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: reset command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reset a failed or partial epic so I can re-run it.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents reset EPIC-001 sets the epic status back to pending
Also resets all userStories passes to false within that epic
Shows confirmation of what was reset
Shows error if epic ID not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: add-epic command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add a new epic to an existing prd.json without recreating everything.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents add-epic [path] starts interactive prompts for a new epic
Asks for: epic title, description, dependencies (shows existing epic IDs)
Asks to add user stories one at a time
Appends the new epic to the existing prd.json epics array
Auto-generates next epic ID and story IDs (continues from existing)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: validate command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to validate my prd.json before running so I catch errors early.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents validate [path] checks prd.json structure
Validates: required fields exist, IDs are unique, dependsOn references valid epic IDs, no circular dependencies
Prints all errors found (doesn't stop at first)
Exits 0 if valid, 1 if errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: summary command with dependency tree
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a visual overview of my epics and their dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph-team-agents summary [path] shows a tree view of epics
Shows dependency arrows between epics (e.g. EPIC-001 → EPIC-002)
Shows story count and pass rate per epic
Highlights blocked epics (deps not met)
Uses chalk colors for status
Typecheck passes
